hero.recraft_prompt_if_needed: Professional Black male American mobile mechanic working under the hood of a car in a residential driveway, tool belt visible, wearing clean work uniform, photorealistic, no text or logos, no signage.
hero.caption_context: Robert personally is the brand — prefer a photo of him in action over generic mechanic shots. Black-owned positioning is a distinctive angle; hero should reflect it authentically.

gallery.context: Engine diagnostics with scan tool — check engine light scenario. Caption 'Diagnostics — check engine, drivability, fault codes'.
Brake pad replacement or rotor service. Caption 'Brakes — pads, rotors, full replacement'.
Battery replacement or alternator test. Caption 'Batteries + alternators — tested, replaced'.
Transmission service (rare mobile specialty). Caption 'Transmission + major mechanical — mobile, same bay'.
Body repair or window service. Caption 'Body + windows — full-scope mobile'.
Robert personally or his service van. Caption names Robert — reinforces owner-operator brand.

sections.context: Donald Lee Hollowell / West Atlanta streetscape; Unsplash OK if no real photos

notes: Mobile auto repair = owner-operator trust is the brand. Black-owned is a distinctive positioning — surface authentically, not tokenly. Broad mechanical scope (transmission + body) is the differentiator from oil-change-only mobile shops. If Bruce surfaces job-completion photos (finished brake jobs, etc.), those are high-conversion for the gallery.
